libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required for baseline meaningful tests. Existing javac failures are due to Maven 2.0.7 API signature drift in maven-artifact exception constructors, so tests should use constructors available in 2.0.7 rather than copied 2.0.6-era assumptions. If tests exercise full Maven execution rather than non-network APIs or MavenCli --help/--version, keep any project, settings, and local-repository paths temporary and self-contained.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8771 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven:maven-core:2.0.7; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Full MavenCli execution can read settings, inspect user.dir, and attempt plugin/artifact resolution; generated tests should avoid external network-dependent builds unless they fully isolate inputs.
This is a very old Maven 2 artifact, so constructor signatures and behavior differ from later Maven versions and from generated assumptions based on nearby versions.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: org.apache.maven:maven-core:2.0.7 resolves its required Maven/Plexus/ClassWorlds/Wagon dependencies transitively, and meaningful coverage is reachable through Maven core APIs, bundled resources, diagnosers, lifecycle constants, runtime information, and MavenCli --help/--version without Docker, extra Maven dependencies, or required environment/system properties.
